sins, and in his fine delineation of the features of Lord Visnu, which
is on a par with his Dhyana-slokaA
He was a psychologist par excellence too. He was a keen
observer of external nature as well as human nature and knew the
working of the human mind. His ethical standard was very high and
his instructions on moral conduct are worth their weight in gold.5
1. Vide XLIII. 38; XLVIII. 31; LIX. 8; XCV. 20; XII. 17.
2. „ XLVIII. 81.
3. „ XLVI. 3; XLVIII. 3, 82-86; CIV. 48.
4. „ XII. 14; LVIII. 31-35.
5. „ LXXV. 6-10.

xxxvi Brhat Samhitd
Commentaries
It is presumed that the Brhat Samhitd, which had attained
great fame in olden days, had many scholarly commentaries. It is a
well-known fact that scholarship was measured in India in terms of
original writings and scholarly commentaries on famous works.
However, we have no knowledge of any commentary on this work
prior to that of Bhaffotpala, from whose references we can conclude
